NCT.evidence: Found in the "Methods" section on page 1 and "Statistical analysis" section on page 3, explicitly stating the trial registration number.

Author.value: Thomas E Hutson, Vladimir Lesovoy, Salman Al-Shukri, Viktor P Stus, Oleg N Lipatov, Angel H Bair, Brad Rosbrook, Connie Chen, Sinil Kim, Nicholas J Vogelzang
Author.evidence: Found listed as authors on page 1, directly below the article title.

Year.evidence: Found in the publication details on page 1, "Lancet Oncol 2013".

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document contains extensive sections including Summary, Background, Methods, Findings, Interpretation, Introduction, Results, Discussion, and References, indicating it is a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: Stated in the title and confirmed in the "Methods" section on page 2 as "a randomised open-label phase 3 trial".

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The background and introduction state that the trial was undertaken to compare axitinib with sorafenib in "treatment-naive metastatic renal-cell carcinoma" and reports results from the "treatment-naive population", indicating it's an original study for this patient group.

Number of Arms.evidence: The "Methods" section on page 1 states patients were randomly assigned to receive "axitinib 5 mg twice daily, or sorafenib 400 mg twice daily", indicating two treatment arms.

Treatment Arm.evidence: The title of the article is "Axitinib versus sorafenib...", and the "Methods" section on page 1 describes patients receiving "axitinib 5 mg twice daily".

Treatment Arm 2.evidence: The schema includes "Treatment Arm 2" and "Control Arm". The study compares Axitinib to Sorafenib, where Sorafenib acts as the comparator/control. The document does not explicitly label Sorafenib as "Treatment Arm 2" in addition to being the control.

Control Arm.evidence: The "Methods" section on page 1 states patients were randomly assigned to receive "axitinib 5 mg twice daily, or sorafenib 400 mg twice daily", with sorafenib serving as the comparator.

Type of Agent in Treatment Arm.evidence: Found in the "Panel: Research in context" section on page 7, describing axitinib as "a VEGF-receptor inhibitor".

Is study included in Meta-analysis.evidence: The 'Panel: Research in context - Systematic review' on page 7 states, 'A systematic literature review was not done as part of the planning for this trial.' The 'Background' and 'Methods' sections on page 1 describe a single phase 3 trial.

Is OS data available.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 2 under 'Procedures' lists 'overall survival' as a secondary endpoint. The 'Statistical analysis' section on page 3 mentions, 'Final analysis of overall survival requires 178 deaths; these data are currently immature. Patients are being followed up for survival every 3 months after study treatment discontinuation.' The 'Discussion' section on page 7 also confirms that overall survival data is being collected and will be reported.

Is QoL data available.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 2 under 'Procedures' lists 'patient-reported outcomes' as a secondary endpoint and specifies, 'Assessments used the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Kidney Symptom Index (FKSI)-15, FKSI-Disease Related Symptoms (FKSI-DRS) subscale, and European Quality of Life-5 Dimensions (EQ-5D) questionnaires.' The 'Statistical analysis' section on page 3 and 'Discussion' section on page 7 also discuss patient-reported outcomes and quality of life.

The number of arms.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states, 'patients... were randomly assigned (2:1)... to receive axitinib 5 mg twice daily, or sorafenib 400 mg twice daily.' This is further confirmed in 'Methods - Randomisation and masking' on page 2 and 'Figure 1: Trial profile' on page 4, which shows '192 assigned to axitinib' and '96 assigned to sorafenib'.

Risk.evidence: 'Table 1: Patient demographic and baseline characteristics' on page 3 includes 'MSKCC risk group (number of risk factors)' with categories Favourable (0), Intermediate (1-2), and Poor (≥3). The 'Statistical analysis' section on page 3 also mentions 'Memorial Sloan-Kettering Cancer Center [MSKCC] risk group' as a characteristic explored for its potential influence on progression-free survival.

Favorable Risk PFS | Rx (mo).evidence: The document mentions MSKCC risk groups (Favourable, Intermediate, Poor) in Table 1 and Figure 3, but does not provide median Progression-Free Survival (PFS) in months specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' group for the Axitinib (Rx) arm.

Favorable Risk PFS | Control (mo).evidence: The document mentions MSKCC risk groups (Favourable, Intermediate, Poor) in Table 1 and Figure 3, but does not provide median Progression-Free Survival (PFS) in months specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' group for the Sorafenib (Control) arm.

Favorable Risk ORR Rx | Overall.evidence: The document provides overall Objective Response Rate (ORR) in Table 2, but does not provide ORR (Overall) specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' group for the Axitinib (Rx) arm.

Favorable Risk ORR Rx | CR.evidence: The document provides overall Complete Response (CR) in Table 2, but does not provide CR specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' group for the Axitinib (Rx) arm.

Favorable Risk ORR Rx | PR.evidence: The document provides overall Partial Response (PR) in Table 2, but does not provide PR specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' group for the Axitinib (Rx) arm.

Favorable Risk ORR Control | Overall.evidence: The document provides overall Objective Response Rate (ORR) in Table 2, but does not provide ORR (Overall) specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' group for the Sorafenib (Control) arm.

Favorable Risk ORR Control | CR.evidence: The document provides overall Complete Response (CR) in Table 2, but does not provide CR specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' group for the Sorafenib (Control) arm.

Favorable Risk ORR Control | PR.evidence: The document provides overall Partial Response (PR) in Table 2, but does not provide PR specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' group for the Sorafenib (Control) arm.

Int-Poor Risk PFS | Rx (mo).evidence: The document mentions MSKCC risk groups (Intermediate, Poor) in Table 1 and Figure 3, but does not provide median Progression-Free Survival (PFS) in months specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' group for the Axitinib (Rx) arm.

Int-Poor Risk PFS | Control (mo).evidence: The document mentions MSKCC risk groups (Intermediate, Poor) in Table 1 and Figure 3, but does not provide median Progression-Free Survival (PFS) in months specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' group for the Sorafenib (Control) arm.

Int-Poor Risk ORR Rx | Overall.evidence: The document provides overall Objective Response Rate (ORR) in Table 2, but does not provide ORR (Overall) specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' group for the Axitinib (Rx) arm.

Int-Poor Risk ORR Rx | CR.evidence: The document provides overall Complete Response (CR) in Table 2, but does not provide CR specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' group for the Axitinib (Rx) arm.

Int-Poor Risk ORR Rx | PR.evidence: The document provides overall Partial Response (PR) in Table 2, but does not provide PR specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' group for the Axitinib (Rx) arm.

Int-Poor Risk ORR Control | Overall.evidence: The document provides overall Objective Response Rate (ORR) in Table 2, but does not provide ORR (Overall) specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' group for the Sorafenib (Control) arm.

Int-Poor Risk ORR Control | CR.evidence: The document provides overall Complete Response (CR) in Table 2, but does not provide CR specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' group for the Sorafenib (Control) arm.

Int-Poor Risk ORR Control | PR.evidence: The document provides overall Partial Response (PR) in Table 2, but does not provide PR specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' group for the Sorafenib (Control) arm.

N | Treatment Arm.evidence: Found in the "Findings" section on page 1, stating "we randomly assigned 192 patients to receive axitinib", and in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "Axitinib (n=192)".

N | Control Arm.evidence: Found in the "Findings" section on page 1, stating "and 96 patients to receive sorafenib", and in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "Sorafenib (n=96)".

Median Follow Up Duration (mo).evidence: The "Results" section on page 4 explicitly states, "Data are not available for median follow-up."

Median Follow Up Duration for QoL (mo).evidence: The document discusses patient-reported outcomes and quality of life instruments but does not report a specific median follow-up duration for quality of life.

Sex | Males(%).evidence: Found in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "Sex", showing 134 (70%) for axitinib and 74 (77%) for sorafenib.

Sex | Females(%).evidence: Found in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "Sex", showing 58 (30%) for axitinib and 22 (23%) for sorafenib.

Risk Category Criteria.value: MSKCC risk groups were classified on the basis of four risk factors: high lactate dehydrogenase (>1·5xupper limit of normal), low serum haemoglobin (<lower limit of normal), high corrected serum calcium (>2·5 mmol/L), and absence of previous nephrectomy.
Risk Category Criteria.evidence: Found in the footnote of Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3.

Risk Category Included.evidence: Found in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "MSKCC risk group (number of risk factors)".

HrQoL Instrument.value: Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Kidney Symptom Index (FKSI)-15, FKSI-Disease Related Symptoms (FKSI-DRS) subscale, and European Quality of Life-5 Dimensions (EQ-5D) questionnaires.
HrQoL Instrument.evidence: Found in the "Procedures" section on page 2, stating "Assessments used the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Kidney Symptom Index (FKSI)-15, FKSI-Disease Related Symptoms (FKSI-DRS) subscale, and European Quality of Life-5 Dimensions (EQ-5D) questionnaires."

PRO Completion Rate (%) | Baseline.evidence: Found in the "Results" section on page 5, stating "Completion numbers for patient-reported outcome questionnaires at baseline and throughout the trial were high (>95%)".

PRO Completion Rate (%) | On Rx.evidence: Found in the "Results" section on page 5, stating "Completion numbers for patient-reported outcome questionnaires at baseline and throughout the trial were high (>95%)".

Risk Category (%) | Favorable.evidence: Found in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "MSKCC risk group (number of risk factors)", showing 94 (49%) for axitinib and 53 (55%) for sorafenib.

Risk Category (%) | Intermediate.evidence: Found in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "MSKCC risk group (number of risk factors)", showing 84 (44%) for axitinib and 40 (42%) for sorafenib.

Risk Category (%) | Poor.evidence: Found in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "MSKCC risk group (number of risk factors)", showing 7 (4%) for axitinib and 2 (2%) for sorafenib.

Nephrectomy (%) | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "Previous nephrectomy", showing 164 (85%) for axitinib.

Nephrectomy (%) | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "Previous nephrectomy", showing 86 (90%) for sorafenib.

Organs Involved at Baseline (%) | Lymph.evidence: Found in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "Sites of metastasis", showing 99 (52%) for axitinib and 55 (57%) for sorafenib.

Organs Involved at Baseline (%) | Liver.evidence: Found in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "Sites of metastasis", showing 52 (27%) for axitinib and 25 (26%) for sorafenib.

Organs Involved at Baseline (%) | Lung.evidence: Found in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "Sites of metastasis", showing 137 (71%) for axitinib and 72 (75%) for sorafenib.

Organs Involved at Baseline (%) | Bone.evidence: Found in Table 1 "Patient demographic and baseline characteristics" on page 3, under "Sites of metastasis", showing 56 (29%) for axitinib and 24 (25%) for sorafenib.

Organs Involved at Baseline (%) | Brain.evidence: The document mentions brain scans for baseline tumor assessment but does not provide a percentage for brain involvement at baseline in Table 1 "Sites of metastasis" or elsewhere.

Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: Found in the 'Methods' section on page 1 and 'Procedures' section on page 2, explicitly stating 'The primary endpoint was progression-free survival'.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: objective response, response duration, overall survival, safety, and patient-reported outcomes
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: Found in the 'Procedures' section on page 2, explicitly listing the 'Secondary endpoints'.

OS (mo) | Rx.evidence: The 'Discussion' section on page 7 states, 'median overall survival had not been reached'.

OS (mo) | Control.evidence: The 'Discussion' section on page 7 states, 'median overall survival had not been reached'.

PFS (mo) | Rx.evidence: Found in the 'Findings' section on page 1 and 'Results' section on page 4, and Figure 2A on page 5, stating '10.1 months (95% CI 7.2-12.1)' for axitinib.

PFS (mo) | Control.evidence: Found in the 'Findings' section on page 1 and 'Results' section on page 4, and Figure 2A on page 5, stating '6.5 months (4.7-8.3)' for sorafenib.

ORR (%) Rx | Overall.evidence: Found in the 'Results' section on page 4 and Table 2 on page 6, stating '62 (32%) of 192' patients had an objective response (which is the sum of CR and PR, and CR was 0).

ORR (%) Rx | CR.evidence: Found in the 'Results' section on page 4 and Table 2 on page 6, explicitly stating 'No patient had a complete response with either treatment' and '0' for Axitinib.

ORR (%) Rx | PR.evidence: Found in Table 2 on page 6, stating '62 (32%)' for partial response in the axitinib group.

ORR (%) Control | Overall.evidence: Found in the 'Results' section on page 4 and Table 2 on page 6, stating '14 (15%) of 96' patients had an objective response (which is the sum of CR and PR, and CR was 0).

ORR (%) Control | CR.evidence: Found in the 'Results' section on page 4 and Table 2 on page 6, explicitly stating 'No patient had a complete response with either treatment' and '0' for Sorafenib.

ORR (%) Control | PR.evidence: Found in Table 2 on page 6, stating '14 (15%)' for partial response in the sorafenib group.

All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher (%) | Rx.evidence: The document lists individual all-cause Grade 3 and Grade 4 adverse events in Table 3 and in the text, but does not provide a single overall percentage for 'All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher (%)' for the Rx group.

All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher (%) | Contro.evidence: The document lists individual all-cause Grade 3 and Grade 4 adverse events in Table 3 and in the text, but does not provide a single overall percentage for 'All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher (%)' for the Control group.

TRAE Grade 3 or Higher (%) | Rx.evidence: The document provides percentages for 'treatment-related serious adverse events' (5%) and lists individual 'treatment-emergent, all-causality adverse events' by grade in Table 3, but does not provide an overall percentage for 'TRAE Grade 3 or Higher (%)' for the Rx group.

TRAE Grade 3 or Higher (%) | Control.evidence: The document provides percentages for 'treatment-related serious adverse events' (7%) and lists individual 'treatment-emergent, all-causality adverse events' by grade in Table 3, but does not provide an overall percentage for 'TRAE Grade 3 or Higher (%)' for the Control group.

TRAE Grade 5 (%) | Rx.evidence: The footnote for Table 3 on page 6 explicitly states 'none were grade 5' for treatment-emergent, all-causality adverse events.

TRAE Grade 5 (%) | Control.evidence: The footnote for Table 3 on page 6 explicitly states 'none were grade 5' for treatment-emergent, all-causality adverse events.

No of Deaths | Rx.evidence: Found in the 'Articles' section on page 5, stating 'At data cutoff, 78 (41%) of 192 patients in the axitinib group ... had died'.

No of Deaths | Control.evidence: Found in the 'Articles' section on page 5, stating '...and 36 (38%) of 96 in the sorafenib group had died'.

Significant QoL Benefit for Rx.evidence: The 'Discussion' section on page 7 states that 'quality of life worsened at the end of treatment with both axitinib and sorafenib' and 'No significant differences were noted between axitinib and sorafenib in median time-to-deterioration composite endpoints', indicating no significant QoL benefit for Rx over control.
